name: Sales Orders to Customer Deposits / Invoices Add
description: Use this flow as a base for creating Customer Deposits from Sales Orders

accountID.name: accountID
accountID.label: Account Internal ID
accountID.helpText: Which is the account ID for the Customer Deposit?

currencyID.name: currencyID
currencyID.label: Currency Internal ID
currencyID.helpText: Which is the currency ID for the Customer Deposit?

aiDescription.summary: The "Sales Orders to Customer Deposits / Invoices Add" flow is used to create customer deposits from sales orders. It runs every 30 minutes starting from the 5th minute of each hour in the America/Los_Angeles timezone. The flow has two parts: "pageGenerators" and "pageProcessors". The "pageGenerators" section exports sales order data from NetSuite using a RESTlet. The "pageProcessors" section has two destinations: "Add Customer Deposit" and "Create Invoice from Sales Order". Both imports use a preMap hook function to modify the data before mapping. The flow also has a settings form for configuring account and currency IDs. It has the "autoResolveMatchingTraceKeys" option enabled to resolve errors.
aiDescription.detailed:
This flow is named "Sales Orders to Customer Deposits / Invoices Add" and is used as a base for creating customer deposits from sales orders. It has a schedule set to run every 30 minutes starting from the 5th minute of each hour in the America/Los_Angeles timezone.

The flow consists of two parts: "pageGenerators" and "pageProcessors". 

In the "pageGenerators" section, there is one source called "Get Sales Orders". It exports data from NetSuite using a RESTlet. The RESTlet is configured to export sales order records based on a saved search with the search ID "1845". The exported data is not grouped by internal ID. The export is not limited to stats only, and the batch size for marking exported records is set to 100.

In the "pageProcessors" section, there are two destinations. The first destination is an import called "Add Customer Deposit". It imports data into NetSuite as customer deposit records. The import is distributed, meaning it is split into multiple batches for faster processing. The import uses a preMap hook function to modify the data before mapping. The hook function adds the account ID and currency ID to each record. The internal ID lookup expression is used to check if a customer deposit record already exists based on the external ID. Existing records are ignored, and missing data is not ignored.

The second destination is another import called "Create Invoice from Sales Order". It imports data into NetSuite as invoice records. Similar to the previous import, this import is also distributed and uses a preMap hook function. The internal ID lookup expression is used to check if an invoice record already exists based on the external ID. Existing records are ignored, and missing data is not ignored.

The flow also has a settings form that allows users to configure the account ID and currency ID for the customer deposits. These settings are used in the preMap hook function.

The flow has the "autoResolveMatchingTraceKeys" option enabled, which resolves other open errors with the same trace key.

Overall, this flow exports sales order data from NetSuite, processes it to create customer deposit and invoice records in NetSuite, and allows for customization through the preMap hook functions.
